fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Massachusetts Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Massachusetts - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity massachusetts, how to transfer physical therapist license to massachusetts, physical therapist compact massachusetts, massachusetts physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Massachusetts Board of Allied Health — PT
board.mailing_address: Board of Registration in Allied Health Professions, 1000 Washington Street, Suite 710, Boston, MA 02118

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 30 contact hours of continuing education per renewal cycle

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Massachusetts.

steps.title: Create a MassLicensing Account
steps.description: Navigate to the MassLicensing portal at https://masslicensing.portal.fidlar.com/login and register for a new user account using a valid email address.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your MassLicensing account, select 'Apply for a New License' and choose the Physical Therapist application. Fill out all required sections of the application form.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents through the MassLicensing portal, including official transcripts from your PT program, NPTE scores (sent directly from FSBPT), and verification of licensure from other states (if applicable).

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $180.00 through the MassLicensing portal as part of your application submission.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Board of Registration in Allied Health Professions will review your complete application and all submitted documentation. Once approved, your license will be issued and viewable on your MassLicensing account and the MA license verification system.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Massachusetts physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $180. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Massachusetts have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers physical therapist practice in Massachusetts. To work in Massachusetts, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Massachusetts?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Massachusetts while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Massachusetts
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Massachusetts, you must create an account and submit your application through the online MassLicensing portal.
